Phacelia (Phacelia spp.) is a genus of flowering plants in the borage family (Boraginaceae), known for its attractive flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Phacelia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Phacelia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
